commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then they told David, saying, Behold, the Philistines fight against Keilah, and they rob the threshing floors. The floors were composed of earth and the dregs of oil, made into a sort of mortar, so that rain, mice, could not hurt them. (Cato 91 and 129. ) Hither the people of Ceila had gathered their corn, and the enemy came to plunder, or to spoil, according to custom, Judges v. (Calmet) Ceila was about seven miles from Hebron, and as many from Eleuthero polis.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
